name: Chicago Post Office Employees
description_short: Chicago Post Office Employees Credit Union is a state-chartered credit union established in 1928, serving over 4,900 members with $29.2M in assets through 1 branch and 3 ATMs.
description_long:
Chicago Post Office Employees Credit Union was established in 1928 and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. As a state-chartered institution, it has grown to serve a loyal member base of over 4,900 individuals with total assets of $29.2 million. The credit union operates with 11 dedicated employees and maintains a single physical branch location at 10027 S Western Avenue, supplemented by 3 ATMs for member convenience.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, and mortgage products. Members can access bill pay, online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, and e-statements through digital channels. The institution also provides wire transfers, financial calculators, and various educational resources including financial counseling, financial education workshops, and student scholarships.

Chicago Post Office Employees Credit Union distinguishes itself through its long operational history spanning nearly a century and its focus on serving the specific community of post office employees in the Chicago area. The credit union provides electronic signature authentication and maintains an online banking platform with a dedicated mobile app available on both iOS and Android. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, it operates on a member-owned cooperative model.

However, the institution faces significant service challenges based on recent member feedback. Multiple reviews cite poor customer service, including difficulty obtaining loan information by phone and staff rudeness. The main office lobby is currently closed, limiting in-person service access. The credit union's small size and single branch location may restrict accessibility for members, and negative reviews suggest operational issues with both phone support and online account creation processes.
pros: Established credit union with 95+ years of history and stability (founded 1928)
Offers mortgage loans in addition to standard credit union products
Member-owned cooperative structure typical of credit unions
Provides mobile banking app for iOS and Android devices
Offers financial education, counseling, and student scholarships
Remote deposit capture and online bill pay available
Electronic signature authentication for secure transactions
cons: Member lobby currently closed, limiting in-person service access
Multiple 1-star reviews citing poor customer service and difficulty obtaining loan rates by phone
Single branch location with only 3 ATMs limits accessibility
Technical issues reported with online account creation and phone routing
Small institution ($29.2M assets) may limit product flexibility and service capacity
best_for: Current and retired Chicago Postal Service employees meeting membership eligibility
Members seeking basic banking services with preference for credit union structure
Individuals with existing relationships who can access services primarily through digital channels
diagnosis: Best for Chicago-area postal employees or their families who need basic banking services and prefer the credit union model, but only for those comfortable with digital-first banking due to limited branch hours and documented service issues. The recent negative reviews suggest operational challenges that prospective members should investigate before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
